title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot, Spence, and Eleanor discuss past murder cases to find connections to the recent death of Joyce Reynolds. They first examine the case of Leslie Firrell, a law firm clerk at Fullerton, Harrison & Ladbate who was stabbed from behind near the Green Swan Inn eighteen months ago. Firrell had a criminal record for forgery and possessed a large amount of unexplained cash. Suspects included the innkeeper Harry Griffin, his wife, or members of Firrell's gang. Poirot dismisses this case as unlikely to be related to Joyce.

They then discuss the death of Janet White, a teacher who was strangled on a shortcut two and a half years ago. Her roommate, Nora Ambrose, noted that Janet had been receiving threatening letters from an unnamed man who had broken up with her. Poirot finds this case more relevant, theorizing that Joyce might have witnessed a murder in the past—perhaps Janet's—and only realized the significance of what she saw as she grew older. Poirot suggests that Joyce was killed because she claimed to have seen a murder while preparing for a Halloween party.

Spence provides a list of eighteen people present during the party preparations, including Mrs. Dreyfus, Miss Whitaker, Rev. Charles Cotterell, and the Reynolds family. Spence notes that the list may be incomplete as people frequently arrived and left. Finally, Poirot meets with Dr. Ferguson, a Scottish doctor, who discusses the cruelty of the child's death and the dangers posed by mentally deranged individuals in society.
